gap_observations.extra_observation: psmux list-commands does not expose set-window-option; set-option -w returns 0 but show-window-options reads an empty custom option value.
gap_observations.candidate_workaround: Do not depend on set-window-option; use product-relevant pane title plus sidecar registry, or require a psmux fix that preserves custom option values.
gap_observations.ccb_impact: diagnostics and identity evidence cannot be treated as tmux-equivalent.

gap_observations.extra_observation: Non-interactive probe cannot prove an interactive TUI restore path.
gap_observations.candidate_workaround: Needs an interactive ConPTY/terminal black-box acceptance test before full lifecycle can pass.
gap_observations.ccb_impact: core lifecycle remains unproven because ccb foreground attach is user-visible.

gap_observations.extra_observation: select-pane -T is readable through list-panes, but set-option -p custom pane options return success while #{extra} formats read empty.
gap_observations.candidate_workaround: Use pane title as visible identity and store authoritative slot mapping in ccbd sidecar state.
gap_observations.ccb_impact: Current tmux user-option identity strategy cannot be reused unchanged.

gap_observations.extra_observation: Full probe still lacks real evidence for OSC, wrapping and wide-char fidelity dimensions.
gap_observations.candidate_workaround: Keep provider completion on provider-native/session evidence; use capture-pane only for diagnostics unless parser normalization is strengthened and verified.
gap_observations.ccb_impact: Screen scrape cannot be a sole completion authority.

gap_observations.extra_observation: Manual probe showed load-buffer/paste-buffer can place text into cmd.exe when followed by an explicit Enter; the original probe looked for the marker before the pasted command executed.
gap_observations.candidate_workaround: Model paste as paste-buffer plus explicit submit key or use file/stdin/chunked send for large prompts.
gap_observations.ccb_impact: Large text input appears workable with stricter send semantics, but not yet proven for provider panes.

gap_observations.extra_observation: Manual probe showed C-c interrupts a long-running Python command under PowerShell with KeyboardInterrupt; C-d is rendered as ^D and does not act as EOF.
gap_observations.candidate_workaround: Treat interrupt as supported; do not promise Unix EOF. Use provider-specific quit/submit semantics or Windows EOF alternatives if required.
gap_observations.ccb_impact: Interrupt can be supported; EOF remains non-tmux-equivalent on Windows shells.

overall_current_psmux_conclusion: psmux 3.3.3 is the best current candidate but does not yet pass the full ccb Windows native backend gate without accepted workaround design and an interactive attach test.
